prompt: My NIP-05 verification isn't working. I set nip05 to 'Alice@MyDomain.com' in my profile. My nostr.json file at https://mydomain.com/.well-known/nostr.json contains: {"names":{"Alice":"npub1dp4rcz7ufm4rrequena8s8nh5aykdw9t7e2dj0gya9j9sskjhk0qdjd0lv"}}. When I curl the endpoint, I get a 301 redirect to https://www.mydomain.com/.well-known/nostr.json. What's wrong and how do I fix everything?
assertions: Output identifies that 'Alice' must be lowercase — NIP-05 local-part only allows a-z0-9-_.
Output identifies that the npub pubkey format is wrong and must be converted to 64-character lowercase hex
Output identifies the 301 redirect as a problem — NIP-05 spec requires no HTTP redirects
Output provides a corrected nostr.json with the local-part in lowercase and pubkey in hex format
Output explains that the nip05 field in the profile must also use lowercase (alice@mydomain.com not Alice@MyDomain.com)
Output provides specific guidance on fixing the redirect (e.g., server config change to prevent www redirect on this path)
Output mentions that CORS headers (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *) are required even though the user didn't ask about them
Output does NOT simply say 'convert npub to hex' without explaining that hex is 64 lowercase characters
